items.comment_full: Well. I usually won't leave a review if I don't have a great time. This however I feel needs an explanation.  First of all I don't understand how this has so many great reviews. My girlfriend and I aren't overly picky or demanding but these reviews seem entirely inaccurate. Lets start with the positives.  Beautiful location in the Topanga mountains and the one owner we briefly met was very friendly. Lets move on to the rest. The "Casita" is tiny. The pictures are deceiving.  The bed nearly touches three out of the four walls and sitting on the bed I could touch the 4th. I'm 6'1" and the bed is so short that I had to stick my feet through the bed frame in order to fully stretch out. The toilet and shower are on top of each other with no separation. When you shower, which is behind a closet door and provides no privacy, everything gets wet...the toilet paper, toilet, etc...it's terribly slapped together. The room also had no trash can, but we were provided a bag which we had to sling over a chair. The accessible deck is right outside the master bedroom which makes it unusable at night and the jacuzzi operation was not explained so it was useless to us. There is no internet either. The signal was so weak none of our devices could connect. The owner mentioned he had a booster, but produced no results. The worst was that we came up here for peace and quiet. We could have looked past all this, however at 7AM both Saturday and Sunday we woke up to someone working in their garage with a skill saw right outside our window. I looked to see where the noise was coming from and I could see a garage workshop just over the fence. An overall unpleasant experience.
items.one_line: Bed so tiny guest's feet stick through frame, shower floods toilet

items.comment_full:
“If you are not happy with the place, you can go somewhere else. We are not giving refunds or anything. The people came today to do a deep cleaning...Sorry for the trash and dirty blankets.” Not recommended!!! I have traveled many places and used Airbnb’s and this by far has been the worst experience. Place looks very similar to the pictures minus the first room being a lot smaller than shown.
The place was NOT clean. The concierge ensured the placed was deep cleaned by four people earlier that day, however, I would say otherwise.The linen had stains in both rooms, the bathroom had what appeared to be pizza grease on the toilet paper, bathroom door and bedroom door. Upon arrival, I turned on the lights and there was a roach on the wall and the floor. The chest decor was missing a wheel. The couch had stains, the dishwasher had plastic forks and knives still in there. The back porch had a full bag of trash left and the chair had dirty throw covers and sheets left. The fan had thick lines of dust on them and the filters were not clean.
The back room tv did not have a remote so I used the one from the living room to operate the tv in my room.
We had to buy wash clothes since they only provided bath towels and hand towels.
Since I have never been as dissatisfied with an Airbnb, I did not know the policy to contact Airbnb about the issues I had so I told the host and he did move quickly to get the dirty throw blankets and trash off the porch but when I told him about the other issues, he told me that “If I wasnt happy with the place, I can go somewhere else.” RUDE. The lack luster customer service was not expected and I believe he realized it was rude so he changed his response to “send us pictures of your issues and the owner and you can come to a solution.”
I was more hopeful until I messaged the host the pictures and the host brushed off the situation. He told me that was not a good first impression but that NOLA was basically a swamp and the bugs are inevitable. My sister has stayed in four different Airbnbs in NOLA and has never had bugs. He said they could bring raid (and did not). He also said they could (not would) contact the cleaning company but that I could also contact them as well. Not good customer service because the place reflects you, not the cleaning company only.
There was no welcome book like most places have to welcome guests and give suggestions on places to stay and the house rules; only a stack of wrinkled up papers telling the house rules and how to use the alarm system.
Thankfully, I bring my own linen wherever I go so I used them on top of the stained sheets and when I contacted the cleaning lady, she was very caring and expressed empathy for the situation. She explained that she was not aware of the issues and came to clean what we asked the same day.
Also, the walls are PAPER THIN. I did not believe the review until I experienced it for myself. It literally sounds like the neighbors are walking in the hallways. You can hear their television and footsteps at any moment. Quiet hours start at 10pm but you are pretty much quiet all the time so there are no complaints.
My overall first time in NOLA was amazing. Would I recommend this place, NO THANKS!
items.one_line: Go somewhere else, we won't refund, roaches on walls

items.comment_full:
Don't be deceived by the 4 Star ratings. READ THE REVIEWS in detail and you will find the truth about this place. "Cockroaches" ... "No Parking - Parked in for 2 hours" ... "Cleanliness not great" ... "Hear music all hours" ... "Hot" ... "Three flights of stairs" ... "Informed me after that there was a $30 pet fee".
I understand that people want to be nice, but posting good reviews for dishonest Hosts with dilapidated, stinking accommodations does a disservice to other AirBnB users who end up with a terrible experience, like we had.
Here is the truth about this place (and my first negative review of a Host/Accommodations):
1) The smell of mold and mildew (you can see spots where rain gets into the building and unit) in the unit is only out done by the stench in the entryway staircase. But you will be on the third floor, so it is going to be too hot to open any windows to get fresh air most of the year.
2) There is no elevator! You will be walking up and down three flights of stairs every time you go out.
3) There is no free parking available! There is free parking, but in this area there are so many people (probably a lot of locals) who use the building parking lot to park free while they go and drink that you are VERY unlikely to have a spot. And if you do, it is likely that someone will park in the gravel lot entryway and park you in. There is no sign for "Resident/Guest" parking only, so people clearly know they can use it whenever, and they do.
4) The loud music from the nearby bars will play until closing time, and the drunks walking around being loud can be heard, and loud vehicles "race" up and down the street out front until late into the night trying to look cool and get attention.
5) The area surrounding this building is filled with trash and vomit from the people who walk around eating and getting drunk and throwing their trash on the ground.
6) The furniture in the sitting room has no padding. You will sit down on the couch only to crack your tailbone on the sofa's wooden structure.
7) The appliances are too old and damaged to be sold in a thrift store. The refrigerator door handle is missing.
8) There is a large building being constructed across the street. We didn't have to endure that, because we were there on a Saturday night of a holiday weekend.
The place is nasty, stinky, hot, loud, and there is no place to park. Plenty of nice hotels (some are pet friendly) nearby (with parking garages). We wish we had stayed in one, but we got there too late to change our accommodations and still make it to our show at the Ryman, or we would not have stayed here.
items.one_line: Cockroaches, mold stench, three flights of stairs.

items.comment_full:
Have you ever thought you were in paradise and then you realized it could get even better? Enter Jamie and Erin’s Contemporary Condo (or as I like the call it, Jamie and Erin’s CC by the Sea or the CCSea [patent pending]).
Honestly, I am one of the hardest people to impress ESPECIALLY when it comes to housing (for I’ve had many a bad experiences in the past) however I’m happy to say this was the best vacation find I have found so far.
Long story short, I visited Hawaii for a graduation trip and I, having this curse of wanting to live like royalty, assigned myself to select the sleeping location because my friends were going to pitch a tent and call it good... no no good people reading this review, that would not do... not for me in the slightest. And thus I began hours of research, finding a location where I would feel comfortable at.
Look, let’s get straight to the point here. A condo location on the North Shore of Hawaii? Are there bugs/cockroaches?
Ive had my fair share of horror stories involving cockroaches the size of mice running across my kitchen table.. and in my clothes dryer... and on my head boards when a wake up... So... this was a real concern for me.
I’m happy to say, Jamie and Erin’s CCSea passed with flying colors.
There are NO bugs/cockroaches at the CCSea.
As long as you listen to their rules, guidelines, and suggestions, you will have nothing to worry about. (To summarize rules/guidelines/suggestions, don’t leave the kitchen a mess and take out the garbage every night)
Now back to my first impression of the place...
When we pulled up and I open the door to take a peek inside... chills... the good kind of chills. The “Everything I could have wanted and more” kind of chills.
Beautiful kitchen that’s fully stocked with all your fun little kitchen gadgets, plates, silverware, etc..
Chic living room area with a SUPER comfy couch that still has the good “new” smell to it with a crisp TV mounted on the wall.
The master bed is MASSIVE and comfortable. (Not too hot or too cold - Good quality mattress)
The bathroom... oh my... now don’t get me wrong, I shower regularly, but I’ve never been more excited to take a shower before!
I turned back towards my friends who all had their jaws dropped, eyebrows raised, and had the corners of their mouth starting to form into a gentle grin (yup, just like the movies) saying, “Okay, I’m not leaving”
And that’s the thing, I only spent 3 nights here (should have booked my entire trip here) and I already feel like it’s a second home to me.
So for any of you that are hesitant on a non-hotel vacation. Then I’m happy to say you can rest at ease if you stay at Jamie and Erin’s CCSea (patent pending). It’s very hard to please me (mom taught me what I know) and I am overjoyed to say that this place is a winner and one I would recommend it to all my friends (as well as my mother)
And there you have it. A little piece of paradise inside of paradise. Thank you Jamie and Erin for an unforgettable adventure (the good kind). I’ll come to visit again soon.
items.one_line: Royalty expectations meet North Shore reality check

items.comment_full:
I think Jonathan is a highly responsive host who cares about his property.
First and foremost, the house is right next to (as in, only a couple of steps away from) active train tracks. I’m shocked that no one has mentioned this in prior reviews; it’s a huge deal but it isn’t mentioned anywhere. I couldn’t sleep at all. The house shakes whenever a train passes and it’s very loud. I’m a light sleeper so the noise and constant movement woke me up throughout the night and it was very hard for me to even fall asleep. In addition, there’s some type of loud buzzing noise that happens every half hour; almost like a dryer buzzer; we thought perhaps it was the ice maker since it comes from the kitchen area. For a light sleeper like me, it’s very hard to sleep through.
The neighborhood is mediocre; very residential. Some run down homes and some nice houses. If you’re vacationing and looking to walk/take in sights, it’s not the most scenic place to stay but it is indeed close to the airport and just about 15 mins from the beach. So, it’s a quick drive to most places you’re trying to get to. Very convenient.
The house is probably fine to stay in for one night but we rented it for a week, vacationing for the holiday. I don’t think it’s that kind of place for a family. We found that there was no cable TV, so it’s not necessarily a place you want to sit and lounge around if you’re vacationing; no dishwasher, so you’ll be washing dishes constantly if you don’t buy paper goods.
We couldn’t use the washer during our 6-day stay, which made it impossible to wash towels and linens. The house having a washer/dryer was one of the factors that went into choosing it because we were traveling with kids. The host offered to reimburse us up to $50 for our laundry IF we provided a receipt. That’s a little crazy. First off, what laundromat provides receipts? Second, wouldn’t you just refund your guests for any amenity that you claim exists in your home if it doesn’t really exist or work? We had to go to the laundromat.
The house was not cleaned well. On the surface, it looked clean but it definitely wasn’t cleaned well. We found uneaten food in the oven, the microwave had food specs and there were hair/bath products on the shower wall. It’s not really a big deal except that the guest pays a cleaning fee for a house that wasn’t cleaned well to begin with.
We noticed that none of the windows or doors were locked when we got in the first night after being out for dinner. If safety is a concern for you, check for that type of thing.
The beds were fine. I would say that the pillows need to be replaced but overall, the bed was fine.
We’ve traveled quite a bit in 2021 and many of the hotels we’ve stayed in don’t book the room out for 24 hours after a guest leaves because they want to clean and let the room air out. So, I think it’s a bit much to turn a house over in a couple of hours after a guest leaves.
Unfortunately, the guests before us damaged the walls and broke a glass window on an interior door - so we didn’t get the house in the best condition and the hosts had no time to make repairs because we checked in a few hours after they checked out.
Overall, the house is fine but for a certain type of guest. I am a stickler for cleanliness, practicality (washer/dryer/dishwasher/extra linens/cable TV) and aesthetics (location/decor, etc). The house was just mediocre in those ways but very quaint, otherwise. However, the host is professional and responsive. If he knows your needs in advance, I think he would try to accommodate them.
items.one_line: House next to active train tracks; shaking and roaring nightly

items.comment_full:
This apartment is lovely, nicely appointed with great dishes and cutlery, something that you don’t always find in rental apartments. It also had nice appliances and the view is beautiful. Having said that, we wouldn’t stay here again and due to no fault of the host but the location. First, you’re in the direct flight path of the airport which is no super big deal because the planes will eventually stop at night but the bigger issue is they are located right next to a park that has free range roosters and peacocks. Dozens of these loud birds. Those birds start making noise at 2 AM, not kidding. What happened to waiting until sunrise Mr. rooster?! One time it started at midnight but they quieted it down and waited until 3 AM to start the full on chorus of rooster crowing and it didn’t stop. Seriously, it did not stop. Despite us having a very comfortable bed and nice linens and pillows, when these birds started it was almost impossible to sleep, they are that loud and like I said, there is nothing you can do besides kill all those roosters and peacocks which is something we thought about every single night! All joking aside, I’d rather take a chance on the downtown locations where the drunks are usually asleep by 3 AM but to be awakened every night by these birds was crazy.
Also, the picture that shows that open viewpoint is the living room with windows that open, to us it looked like a covered terrace so I didn’t want anyone else to be confused like we were. Three of the four windows did not open because they’re being replaced, no problem there, but the terrace is 3 feet wide by probably 15 or 20 feet long alongside the bedrooms so you have a completely different view which also is nice but I did think the terrace was more the picture that they showed. Another note, the “arm chair“ is a leather chair that was incredibly uncomfortable. If you’re a kid it’s probably a blast because the minute you sit in it you slide out of it but as an adult it sucked. I had to stack up those two little beanbag foot stools they have and then drop a couple pillows into the chair so that I wouldn’t slide out while watching TV. By the way, they have a great HDTV TV set up with one mounted on the living room wall and another in the other bedroom that we did not use.
Fast Wi-Fi was a plus, they will give you the code when you check in so write it down in case you forget, it would be nice to have a house manual that listed some of that stuff. Also, when we checked in and asked the gentleman about grocery stores he seemed perplexed and told me it was a residential area to which I replied that the residents have to have a store to shop in, right? We found two grocery stores 10 to 12 minutes walk from the apartment, the first one is 10 and keep on going and the next one is just around the corner. There are also public buses available depending on which direction you go from the apartment so that’s nice to not have to rely on Uber which is wonderfully cheap but it’s nice to see the city from buses and the delightful trams and a €6.95 daily pass is great (buy it in a metro station)!
We have visited Lisbon many many times and loved the city and as much as we loved the view from this gorgeous apartment it wouldn’t be for us in the future.
items.one_line: Roosters and peacocks screech starting at midnight continuously
